english: Good morning, how did you sleep?
gikuyu: Wîmwega ruciinî. Uraire atîa?

english: Where are you going this afternoon?
gikuyu: Ûrathiî kû mîaraho ya ûmûthî?

english: Please greet your family from me.
gikuyu: Ngeithîria andû aku

english: I have not seen you in a long time.
gikuyu: Ndikuonete kahinda karaihu

english: Thank you for coming to visit us today.
gikuyu: Ngatho nî gûûka gûtucerera
notes: Alt: Ngatho nî ûndû wa gûûka gûtûcerera

english: How are your children doing in school?
gikuyu: Twana twaku no tûrathoma wega cukuru?

english: We will meet again tomorrow at the market.
gikuyu: Nîtûkonana rîngî rûcio ndûnyû-ini.

english: You look well today, what is your secret?
gikuyu: Ûmûthî ûhana wega, thiri nî kîî?

english: It has been a busy week for everyone here.
gikuyu: Gîkoretwo gî kiumia kîa mîhang'o mûno harî ithuî ithuothe haha.

english: Please sit down and make yourself comfortable.
gikuyu: Ikara thî na ûigue ûiganîire.

english: The rains came early this year and the crops are good.
gikuyu: Mbura nî îraurire tene mwaka ûyû na irio/mîmera nî îrakûra wega

english: We will harvest the maize next week if it does not rain.
gikuyu: Nîtûkagetha mbembe kiumia gîûkîte kwaga kuura.

english: The soil in this valley is very fertile.
gikuyu: Tîîri wa mûkuru ûyû nî mûnoru mûno

english: My mother is cooking beans and sweet potatoes for dinner.
gikuyu: Maitû/mami araruga mboco na ngaci hwaini

english: The goats escaped from the fence last night.
gikuyu: Mbûri ciragerire rûgiri-ini ûtukû

english: We need to plant more vegetables near the river.
gikuyu: Nî twagîrîire tûhande nyeni hakuhî na rûûî

english: The market opens very early in the morning on Saturdays.
gikuyu: Thoko îhingûragwo tene mûno mûthenya wa juma

english: How much does a kilo of tomatoes cost today?
gikuyu: Kilo îmwe ya nyanya îrendio mbeca ciigana ûmûthî?
notes: Alt forms: îrauma atîa / îrendio atîa

english: The tea leaves are ready to be picked this morning.
gikuyu: Macani nomatuuo rûûcini rûrû

english: We keep cows, chickens, and a few sheep on our farm.
gikuyu: Twî na ng'ombe, ngûkû na ng'ondu mûgûnda-inî witû.

english: Take this medicine twice a day after eating.
gikuyu: Ûnyuage dawa îno maita meerî omûthenya warîa irio

english: The child has had a fever for three days now.
gikuyu: Mwana akoretwo na ûrugarî thikû ithatu rîu

english: You should drink plenty of clean water every day.
gikuyu: O mûthenya ûkanyua maaî maingî matheru

english: This tablet should not be given to pregnant women.
gikuyu: Dawa îno ndîkanyuuo nî mûtumia mûritû

english: The nurse will take your blood pressure before the doctor sees you.
Wash your hands with soap before and after meals.
gikuyu: Wîthambage momo na thabuni ûtanarîa na warîkia kûrîa

english: Are you allergic to any medicines that you know of?
gikuyu: Nî harî dawa îgûthûkagîra?

english: Malaria is spread through the bites of infected mosquitoes.
gikuyu: Malaria marehagwo ni kûrûmwo ni rwagî

english: Bring the patient back to the clinic if the symptoms get worse.
gikuyu: Ûcokie mûrûarû dariri cia thiî na mbere

english: Immunization protects children from dangerous diseases.
gikuyu: Njanjo nîigiragîrîria ciana kumana na mîrimû ya ûgwati

english: The hospital charges a small fee for outpatient consultations.
gikuyu: Thibitarî ni îrîhagia marîhi manini ma kuona ndagîtarî

english: She has been feeling chest pain and shortness of breath.
gikuyu: Akoretwo akîigua ruo gîthûri na kwaga mîrukî

english: The teacher wrote the lesson on the blackboard.
gikuyu: Mwarimû aandîkire ithoma rûthingo-ini rûirû

english: Students must bring their exercise books every Monday.
gikuyu: Arutwo no mûhaka marehe mabuku mao ma kwandîkîra oo mûthenya wa jumatatû

english: The school fees for this term are due next week.
gikuyu: Marîhi ma cukuru ma ihinda rîrî marîhwo gûgîkinyîria kiumia gîukîte

english: Reading every day helps you to improve your vocabulary.
gikuyu: Gûthoma o mûthenya nîgûteithagia kwagîria rûthiomi rwaku

english: The national exams will be held in November this year.
gikuyu: Igeranio cia bûrûri cigatabanio mweri inî wa ikûmi na ûmwe mwaka ini ûyû

english: She passed all her subjects with very high marks.
gikuyu: Ahîtûkire igeranio ciake ciothe na gíkîro kîa igûrû mûno

english: The school library has many books on science and history.
gikuyu: Nyûmba ya mabuku ya cukuru îrî na mabuku maingi ma thayathi/cayanci na maûndû ma tene

english: Parents are encouraged to attend the school meeting on Friday.
gikuyu: Aciari nîmarorio magoka mucemanio wa cukuru mûthenya wa gatano

english: He wants to study engineering at the university.
gikuyu: Arenda gûthomera injinia cukuru-ini wa yunivasîtî

english: Learning your mother tongue keeps culture alive.
gikuyu: Gûthoma rûthiomi rwa maitû nîgûtûûragia ûndûire

english: My grandmother still remembers all the old stories.
gikuyu: Cûcû witû noaririkanaga ng'ano cia tene

english: The eldest son is responsible for his younger siblings.
gikuyu: Kahîî karîa gakûrû nîko kamenyagîrîra twana tûria twao tûnini
notes: Alt form for older contexts: kîhîî kîrîa gîkûrû

english: We are gathering the whole family for Christmas this year.
gikuyu: Andû othe a mûciî nîtûracemania thigûkû ya kirithimathi mwaka ûyû

english: My uncle lives in Nairobi and visits us during holidays.
gikuyu: Mama witû aikaraga Nairobi na nîokaga gûtûcerera hîndî ya thigûkûû

english: She got married last year and now has a beautiful baby.
gikuyu: Ahikire mwaka ûcio ûrathirire na rîu ena kaana gathaka

english: Children should respect their elders in all circumstances.
gikuyu: Ciana ciathîkagîre akûrû aao hîndî ciothe

english: His father passed away when he was still very young.
gikuyu: Baba wao agîkua kaarî kamwana kanini muno
notes: Alt: baba wao akuire ge kamwana kanini muno

english: Our clan has lived on this land for many generations.
gikuyu: Mûhîrîga wiitu ûikarîte mûgûnda ini ûyû njiarwa nyingî mûno

english: The bride price was agreed upon by both families.
gikuyu: Rûracio nîrûretîkanîirio nî nyûmba/bamîrî cierî

english: We name our children after our grandparents as a tradition.
gikuyu: Twitaga ciana ciitû marîtwa ma maithe maitû o ta mûtugo

english: Send me the document on WhatsApp when you are done.
gikuyu: Ndûmîra iratathi rîu kûgerera WhatsApp warîkia.
notes: Note: no direct Gikuyu word for 'document'; iratathi used

english: My phone battery is dead and I need a charger.
gikuyu: Ihiga rîa thimû yakwa nîrîathira mwaki na nîngwenda chanja

english: I will call you back when I get network signal.
gikuyu: Nîngûkûhûrîra ndakinya handû he na netiwaki

english: You can pay using your mobile money account.
gikuyu: No ûrîhe ûkîhûthîra thimû/akauti ya thimû

english: The internet connection in this area is very poor.
gikuyu: Hinya wa mutambo wa intaneti wî thî mûno gûkû

english: Please enter your PIN number to confirm the transaction.
gikuyu: Hihinya amba yaku ya thiri wîtîkîrie marîhi

english: The television news starts at seven o'clock every evening.
gikuyu: Mohoro ma televisheni manjagîrîria thaa îmwe cia o hwainî

english: She learned how to use a computer at the youth centre.
gikuyu: Erutîire kûhûthîra mûtambo wa kompiuta harîa kambî-inî ya mbeû njîthî

english: Save your work regularly so you do not lose it.
gikuyu: Hingagîra wîra waku maita maingî niguo ndûkoore

english: Download the application on your phone to access the service.
gikuyu: Îkîra ihûthîro rîu thimûini yaku nîgetha waamûkîre ûtungata

english: Every citizen has the right to vote in a free election.
gikuyu: Mûikari o wothe ena rûtha rwa gûikia kura yake gîghuranoiini kîîna waaraganu

english: The county government is building a new health centre in our ward.
gikuyu: Thirikari ya matûûra nî îraka thibitarî njerû itûûra-ini riitû

english: You need a national identity card to access most government services.
gikuyu: Ûbatiî gûkorwo na gîbandî nîguo waamûkîre maûtungata ma thirikari

english: Report any corruption to the relevant authorities immediately.
gikuyu: Thitanga gîîko o gîothe kîa ungumania kûrîa cibatiî

english: The law protects all citizens equally regardless of background.
gikuyu: Watho ûgitagîra aikari othe ûtegûthurania kûrîa mûndû aumîte

english: Register your business to operate legally in this country.
gikuyu: Andîkithia biacara yaku nigetha ûhote kûmîtwara na mbere ûtekuna watho wa bûrûri

english: Public water and electricity are managed by the government.
gikuyu: Maai na thitima cia mûingi ciroragwo ni thirikari

english: The budget for this financial year has been approved by parliament.
gikuyu: Mbajeti ya mwaka ûyû nîyahîtûkio ni nyûmba ya icirîro/mbunge

english: Mount Kenya is the highest mountain in Kenya.
gikuyu: Kîrîma gîa Kenya nîkîo kînene/kîraihu mûno thîinî wa Kenya

english: We must protect the forests to preserve our water sources.
gikuyu: Nomûhaka tûmenyerere mîtitû iitû nîguo tûgitîre njûûî ciitû

english: The dry season has been very long and the rivers are low.
gikuyu: Kîmera kîa riûa nî gîikarîte mûno na njûûî cikahûa

english: Cutting down trees causes flooding during the rainy season.
gikuyu: Gûtema mîtî nîgûtûmaga kûgîe na mwaraho wa kîguû hîndî ya mbura

english: The birds sing loudest just before sunrise in the morning.
gikuyu: Nyoni ciinaga cianîrîire riûa rîtanaratha rûciinî

english: We collect rainwater in tanks for use during dry periods.
gikuyu: Tûigaga maaî ma mbura matangi-inî nîguo tûmahûthîre hîndî ya riûa

english: The fig tree is considered sacred by our community.
gikuyu: Mûtî wa mûgumo nî mûtherû rûrîrî-inî ruitû

english: Climate change is making rainfall patterns unpredictable.
gikuyu: Gûcenjia kwa rîera nîgûtûmîte mbura yaage kwîhokeka

english: I am very happy because my results were excellent.
gikuyu: Ndî na gîkeno mûno tondû maûmîrîra makwa nî meega

english: She felt very sad when she heard the news.
gikuyu: Nîagîire ihooru aigua ûhoro ûcio

english: Do not be afraid, everything will be alright.
gikuyu: Ndûgetigîre, maûndû nîmekwagîra

english: He was very angry when he found out what happened.
gikuyu: Nîarakarire mûno aigua ûrîa kwahanîkire

english: I feel proud of what my children have achieved.
gikuyu: Nîndîretîîra ciana ciakwa nîûndû wa marîa ciîhotanîire

english: She has been feeling lonely since she moved to the city.
gikuyu: Nîakoretwo na ihooru kuuma athamîre mûciî mûnene

english: We were surprised by how quickly the situation changed.
gikuyu: Nîtûragegirio nî ûrîa maûndû macenjetie

english: He is anxious about the test results coming out tomorrow.
gikuyu: Ena wagagu nîûndû wa maumîrîra marîa matanyîîtwo rûciû

english: If it rains tonight, we will not go to the market tomorrow.
gikuyu: Mbura yaura ûmûthî ûtukû, tûtigathiî ndûnyû rûciû

english: Why did you not tell me about this problem earlier?
gikuyu: Waregete kûnjîra ûhoro wa thîna ûcio tene nîkî?

english: When will the bus from Nairobi arrive at the stage?
gikuyu: Mbathibya kuuma Nairobi îgûkinya thitînji thaa cigana?

english: Although she was tired, she still finished all her work.
gikuyu: Ona gûkorwo ararî mûnogu, nîararîkirie wîra wake wothe

english: Which house did you grow up in as a child?
gikuyu: Wakûrîire kû ûnyinyi-inî waku?

english: Tell me what you need and I will try to help you.
gikuyu: Njîra kîrîa ûrabatara nîngûgûteithia

english: He said that he would come but he never arrived.
gikuyu: Augire nîegûka no ndakinyire

english: I do not understand why this problem keeps happening.
gikuyu: Ndiramenya nîkî gîtûmaga thîna ûyû ûgecokera

english: Before you leave, make sure you have everything you need.
gikuyu: Ûtanauma haha rora kana wîna kîrîa gîothe ûbataire

english: How long have you been waiting here for the doctor?
gikuyu: Wetereire ndagîtarî haha kahinda kaigana atîa?

english: Neither the mother nor the father was at home that day.
gikuyu: Nyina kana ithe matiarî mûciî mûthenya ûcio.
notes: Alt: Nyina na ithe gûtirî mûndû warî mûciî mûthenya ûcio.

english: Who is the person responsible for managing this project?
gikuyu: Nûû ûranyabara wîra ûyû?
notes: Note: no specific Gikuyu word for 'project'; alt: nûû ûrûgamîrîire

english: Even though it was difficult, she refused to give up.
gikuyu: Ona gûkorwo nî hararî haritû, nîararegire gûkua ngoro

english: I wish I had known about this opportunity much earlier.
gikuyu: Narî korwo nîndîroî kaanya gaka hau gatene.
